help.pageSubtitle: Documentation for UPG Monitor features. Use the index to jump to a topic or search across all articles.
help.searchEmpty: No articles match your search. Try different words or clear the search.

welcome.title: Welcome to UPG Monitor
welcome.summary: What this app does and how monitors work.
body: UPG Monitor checks your URLs on a schedule and records whether each check succeeded, how long it took, and SSL certificate status when enabled.
You own a private list of monitors. You can share a read-only public status page with visitors using your username.
Use the Dashboard for a quick overview, Monitors to manage configuration, and Activity to see recent up and down events.

signInAndAccount.title: Signing in and registration
signInAndAccount.summary: Login, setup, and who can use the app.
body: Sign in with your email or username and password. If the server was just installed, an administrator creates the first account on the setup page; after that, new users may register if registration is enabled.
Your session keeps you signed in until you sign out or the session expires. You can change your password anytime from Account without losing your session.

dashboardOverview.title: Dashboard overview
dashboardOverview.summary: Summary cards and monitor list at a glance.
body: The Dashboard shows how many monitors are up, down, or paused, and lists every monitor with its current status.
Paused monitors are not checked until you resume them from the Monitors page.
When you have no monitors yet, the Dashboard prompts you to add your first one.

dashboardSearchAndStatusLink.title: Dashboard search and status page link
dashboardSearchAndStatusLink.summary: Find monitors quickly and open your public page.
body: Use the search field to filter monitors by name or URL. Clear the search to show the full list again.
The status page link opens your public page at /status/your-username so you can verify what visitors see.

dashboardTrends.title: Trend charts
dashboardTrends.summary: Recent check history on the Dashboard.
body: Each monitor card can show a small trend of recent checks so you can spot instability without opening the detail page.
Charts reflect stored check results; if a monitor is new, there may be few points until more checks run.

addingMonitors.title: Adding a monitor
addingMonitors.summary: Create a new URL check from the Dashboard or Monitors page.
body: Choose Add monitor and enter a display name and the full URL (including https://). The app will request that URL on your interval.
You can add many monitors; each is checked independently.

monitorSettings.title: Monitor settings explained
monitorSettings.summary: Interval, timeout, method, status codes, alerts, SSL, and status page visibility.
body: Interval is how often checks run. Timeout is the maximum wait for a response before a check is treated as failed.
HTTP method can be GET or HEAD. Expected status codes describe which responses count as success, for example 200-299.
Email alerts notify you when a monitor goes down or recovers; you can override the destination address or use your account default.
SSL monitoring reports certificate issues when enabled. Show on status page controls whether this monitor appears on your public /status/username page.

monitorsListAndBulk.title: Monitors list, sorting, and bulk actions
monitorsListAndBulk.summary: Table controls, selection, pause, resume, edit, and delete.
body: The Monitors table supports sorting by columns such as name, last check, and interval. Search narrows the list by name or URL.
Select one or more monitors to pause, resume, edit shared fields in bulk, or delete. Paused monitors skip scheduled checks.
Deleting a monitor removes its configuration and history from your account; this cannot be undone.

importExportMonitors.title: Import and export monitors
importExportMonitors.summary: JSON backup and restore for monitor definitions.
body: Export downloads a JSON file of your monitors for backup or copying to another environment.
Import accepts a JSON array of monitor objects. Review the preview and confirm; invalid entries are reported without stopping the whole import when possible.

monitorDetailPage.title: Monitor detail page
monitorDetailPage.summary: History, charts, check now, edit, and delete.
body: Open a monitor from the Dashboard or Monitors table to see detailed history, response times, and SSL information when enabled.
Check now runs an immediate check outside the normal schedule. Edit and Delete apply only to this monitor.
Incident and result tables help you see exactly when problems started and ended.

activityFeed.title: Activity feed
activityFeed.summary: Recent up and down events across monitors.
body: Activity lists recent status changes (went down, recovered) within the last several days, with pagination.
Clear all removes stored activity you can see in this view. A dot on the Activity nav item can indicate unread incident-related updates.
Use Activity when you need a chronological story across all monitors instead of per-monitor detail.

accountProfile.title: Account profile and security
body: On Account you can change your display username and interface language. Email is not editable after registration.
Change password requires your current password. After a successful change you remain signed in.

onboardingGuide.title: Onboarding guide
onboardingGuide.summary: Step-by-step introduction vs this Help section.
body: The onboarding guide from the account menu walks you through the product in a short overlay flow and can be reopened anytime.
This Help section is a reference you can search and browse when you need deeper detail on a specific feature.

accountExportImport.title: Account export and import
accountExportImport.summary: Full account backup including monitors and optional history.
body: From Account you can export a JSON file with your profile, monitors, and optionally check history for archival or migration.
Import can merge data or restore in replace mode depending on the options you choose; read the on-screen descriptions carefully before confirming.
Large histories increase file size; you can export without check results if you only need configuration.

publicStatusPage.title: Public status page
body: Your public page lives at /status/your-username. Only monitors with Show on status page enabled appear there.
Visitors do not need to sign in. The page shows high-level status and history appropriate for external communication.

themeAndLanguage.title: Theme and language
body: Use the theme control in the header to switch light and dark mode; your choice is remembered on this device.
Interface language is set on the Account page and applies across the signed-in experience.

pwaOffline.title: Installed app, offline, and refresh
body: You can install UPG Monitor as a PWA from a supporting browser for a more app-like shortcut.
When you are offline, you may see an offline page until connectivity returns. Pull-to-refresh on supported mobile browsers reloads the current view when you are back online.

adminOverview.title: Admin overview
adminOverview.summary: Who admin is for and what the admin area contains.
body: Users with the admin role see an Admin link in the navigation. The admin area is for operating the whole instance, not just your personal monitors.
The overview shows aggregate statistics such as user count, monitor count, and recent check volume.

adminUsers.title: Managing users (admin)
body: Admins can list users and perform administrative actions appropriate to your deployment, such as adjusting roles or access depending on server configuration.
Treat admin capabilities carefully; they affect other people's accounts.

adminMonitorsAndSettings.title: Admin monitors and settings (admin)
body: Admins may have views or tools to inspect monitors and application settings across the installation.
Specific options depend on how your server is configured; use this area when you need instance-level changes rather than edits from a normal user account.
